Item: Pilot sample size is sufficient for stable moderation estimates.
Mitigation: Treat estimates as exploratory and scale scenario count in later runs.

Item: Attack-only design is intentional and aligned with the moderation question.
Mitigation: Interpret results as moderation among attacked individuals, not as a no-attack counterfactual contrast.

Item: Opinion measurement has enough numeric resolution.

Mitigation: Self-supervised realism reviewer + rewrite loop in Stage 03.
Use robust OLS supplement, reduce heterogeneity, and increase scenario count before substantive interpretation.

Item: LLM outputs are reproducible and auditable.
Synthetic LLM agents may not represent human cognition or causal response behavior.
Mitigation: Added transparent assumption register and explicit exploratory caveat in reports.

Item: Adversarial content may be unrealistic or too generic.
Mitigation: Added realism review agent with rewrite loop and heuristic checks.

Item: Model dependence on a single LLM could bias outputs.
Mitigation: Model is CLI-configurable and run metadata captures exact model for replication.

Item: Insufficient sample size inflates uncertainty and may destabilize SEM.
Mitigation: Included robust OLS complement and explicit pilot warnings; ready for scale-up runs.

Item: Attack-only designs cannot estimate the incremental effect of exposure relative to a no-attack counterfactual.
Mitigation: The report states explicitly that the attacked-only design estimates heterogeneity of post-minus-baseline response among attacked individuals only.
